title: Ranunculus imitans
taxonomy: Ranunculus imitans is classified within the family Ranunculaceae. This family, commonly known as the buttercup family, consists of about 2,000 species of plants. Ranunculaceae is divided into about 60 genera, and Ranunculus imitans is one of the species within the Ranunculus genus. The genus Ranunculus is a group of flowering plants that includes about 600 species. Ranunculus imitans is a specific species within this genus.

side_effects: Ranunculus imitans, also known as the false buttercup, contains toxic compounds called glycosides and saponins. Ingestion of the plant can cause symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The toxic compounds can also cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. If ingested in large amounts, Ranunculus imitans can lead to more severe complications, including respiratory failure and cardiac issues. It is essential to exercise caution when handling the plant to avoid any potential side effects.
